Life Math Reflection

Repost: USAMTS r2 reflection

December 3, 2025

Ok, so now that we are done with r2, I just wanted to reflect on it. First of all, here's my thoughts on each problem

p1: Trivial, solved it in under 15 minutes, easiest question I have seen so far.

p2: I misread the question (it seems like many people did), and probably got a zero on it. I should have questioned myself when the question was suddenly so easy, but I convinced myself that p2 is supposed to be this easy.

p3: I don't have much experience with FE's especially with functions like f(f(x)), never seen them in a problem. So I was basically new. However, there was a 50 50 shot if I get the answer or not. The problem was asking if the function exists or doesn't exist, I decided to go with it doesn't exist, and I felt like I made a fairly mathematical argument (lol). I basically limited the function form and definitely got a zero on this problem.

p4: This one was easy. Although I am not really good at geo and failed all my AMCs cuz of geo, this was really straightforward for me and fun to solve. I double checked with geogebra and desmos too lol. Hopefully my proof is good enough and will get me a double digit score on r2

p5: I couldn't solve this one. I had like 1 day to work on it, but figured I would never get it and just gave up on it. I tried doing some research on round robin tournaments and stuff and I saw some interesting stuff but nothing useful came up. None of my approaches worked and was really struggling to sleep cuz of this problem I couldn't solve

Score expectation: 51050(hopefully I hit on something on p2 during my proofs?)

Yeah, it was a rough round, and it was mostly because I first read the problems on the week of Thanksgiving, and stared working on it after thanks giving. So I basically had about 3 days, and I was not too focused in Math during the 3 days either. I was doing sat prep for the December sat, and easily distracted. I probably worked on USAMTS for less than 10 hours. I didn't know misreading a single question would hurt me this much. 5 point seems pretty significant and I am really stupid for not double checking the problem. At this point, I am basically aiming for HM.

For r3, I don't think it is actually worth spending much time on r3 because I have to prepare for AIME. In the case I do make AIME with my scores (88.5 + 4.5 on 12a, idk 12b), I would need about 14 or 15 on AIME to make USAMO. I don't really want to waste 20 hours just on trying to get 25 on USAMTS because there's nothing I can win now except HM. I feel like about 40 should be safe for HM, and I am going to have to see what I get on r1, but if I get like 17 on r1, I only need like 13 on r3 (which is funny to say cuz I will end up dropping less than 13 on r2). If I don't get HM, well, this was my first USAMTS, first proof writing contest, and first actual math contest other than AMC. I am getting experience and learning a lot from doing this. However HM wouldn't be so bad...

Comments
